audit_items.proof_trace: The only pointer operations in checked_delete.hpp are `delete px` and `delete[] x`. In C++, deleting a null pointer is explicitly a no-op per the standard (C++11 §5.3.5/2), so no null pointer dereference can occur. The `sizeof(T)` check is a compile-time type-completeness assertion only — no runtime null check is needed or absent. There are no `*`, `->`, or `[]` dereferences anywhere in the file.
